title: Introduction to Physical Geography
catalogDescription: Analyses the physical structure of the earth's surface, including landforms, weather, climate, and biogeography. Emphasizes understanding of what makes each point on Earth unique and how humans interact with physical systems in multiple ways.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL 99 or instructor consent. Recommended: Eligible for ENGL& 101 .
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: Globalization
catalogDescription: Examines globalization from a geographic perspective, including the cultural and economic effects of rapid communication and transportation networks. Topics include the globalization of popular culture, economic systems, trade agreements, and arguments both for and against globalization.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: Geographic Exploration
catalogDescription: Field course designed to allow students to study the components that make up and contribute to the character of various selected, distinct landscapes while in the field. Skill training (kayak, glacier climbing, scuba, wilderness camping) and subject briefing sessions are usually a part of this course.

grcGeneralEducationCategories.label: Social Science Program Outcomes Identify and describe a current geographic problem or inequity at a global or regional scale. Identify and describe an individual action one can undertake to solve a current geographic problem or inequity.

prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL 99 or instructor consent.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: Environmental Geography
catalogDescription: Studies the relationship between humans and the natural world. Topics include population, consumption, resources, pollution, agriculture, and sustainability. Emphasizes global climate change and the environmental systems of the Puget Sound bioregion.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 and MATH& 141; or instructor consent.

title: Mountain Geography
catalogDescription: Provides a geographic overview of mountains including the importance of mountain regions to humans, the formation, geomorphology, weather, climate, and biology of mountain regions. May include a one day field trip on a Saturday.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.

title: Introduction to Geography

title: Human Geography
catalogDescription: The spatial study of cultural phenomena on earth. Examines the distribution of diverse cultures, ethnicities, languages, religions and economies at a global, as well as regional, scale. Analyzes current geographic problems in the context of migration, population growth, ethnic and religious identities, the colonial legacy and the growing forces of globalization.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.
